delta.comment: Investigation update — repository already contains a local review architecture, so a new generic local PR reviewer would duplicate existing surfaces. Evidence: `templates/skills/review/SKILL.md.tmpl` explicitly supports review before PR creation; `templates/agents/ship.agent.md.tmpl` Step 4.4 invokes local `review` in `mode:report-only` before `pr-lifecycle`; optional `templates/agents/adversarial-review.agent.md.tmpl` and `templates/instructions/adversarial-review.instructions.md.tmpl` provide a higher-confidence local path; current persona catalog includes constitution, language, architecture, concurrency, scope, security, agent-native parity, and learnings researcher. Gap: these personas are generic, not autoharness-bespoke — no reviewer currently specializes in template/frontmatter/placeholder/cross-reference integrity, template-family blast radius, schema+CLI coupling, or docs/template parity. Prior evidence `011.002-T` also shows large-diff/local-review expansion work was considered but not implemented (recommended correctness/maintainability/adversarial/performance/reliability/API/data-migration reviewers are still absent). Recommendation: adopt a hybrid strategy. Keep Copilot for GitHub-native PR/merge gate semantics under P-014, but strengthen the existing local review path so issue-finding shifts earlier, before PR creation. Extend the existing review/persona framework rather than creating a separate reviewer stack. Prioritize autoharness-specific local reviewer coverage for `templates/`, `schemas/`, `src/autoharness/`, `.github/skills/`, `templates/agents/`, and `templates/instructions/`; escalate to adversarial/local-deep review for oversized or multi-family diffs. Do not replace Copilot globally yet — current `pr-lifecycle` and P-014 policy assume it, and replacement would require policy/workflow redesign rather than a simple reviewer addition. Deliberation remains open pending explicit planning of the bespoke local-review extension and its routing thresholds.
